justification: All pass criteria met: conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL is explicitly named; all three agents' positions are attributed (Bird's 100ms rule, MJ's 30s CDN for scalability, Pippen's confirmation of the violation); escalation includes 4 concrete and distinct resolution options (exceeds the 3 required); no brief produced asking Shaq to implement both constraints — output explicitly states 'Do NOT let Shaq start implementation until this is resolved.'

observations.text: Provides 4 resolution paths instead of the minimum 3, each concrete and distinct
Correctly attributes all three agents' positions with specific details (Bird's 100ms/no-stale-reads, MJ's Fastly CDN 30s TTL, Pippen's independent confirmation)
Explicit gate preventing Shaq from starting implementation before resolution
Includes a recommendation with reasoning about which options best balance the constraints
Low confidence score (35) appropriately reflects genuine uncertainty about the ambiguity in Bird's rule scope
Identifies the key clarification needed — whether 100ms applies to all readers or only scoring participants — which is the crux of the conflict
